channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Ancient Egypt-set cozy open-world life simulation game Fields of Aaru announced for PC
item.content:encoded:
Zymartu Games, an independent studio founded by husband-and-wife team Marcel and Thu, has announced Fields of Aaru, a cozy open-world life simulation game set in the afterlife of Ancient Egypt. It has been in development for three years and will launch for PC via Steam in Q4 2026. A release date was not announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via Zymartu Games:

About

A cozy life sim set in the mystical afterlife of Ancient Egypt. Farm along the Nile, befriend the locals, and decorate your eternal home. Venture into the desert and discover oases, pyramids, and tombs. Unlock magical powers by restoring ancient obelisks and making offerings to the gods.

Fields of Aaru drops you into the ancient Egyptian afterlife, where you live out a second life along the Nile. Build a home on the river, help the village by fixing things that have fallen into disrepair, and spend your days farming, fishing, and working the riverbanks where wildlife gathers. When you’re ready, journey into the desert to uncover ancient ruins, tombs, and hidden secrets.

  • ❮
  • ❯

Key Features

  • Befriend the Locals – Meet the villagers, take on quests, and help restore the town. Get to know the people of Aaru and lend a hand through quests that fix up the town and bring daily life back into rhythm. Along the way you’ll learn ancient crafts like shaping stone, firing clay, and irrigating your fields.
  • Harvest the Nile’s Bounty – Build your daily routine around farming, fishing, and gathering. Plant crops in fertile soil, cast a line in the Nile, and head out to gather raw materials you’ll need for tools and building. Use irrigation and shadufs to bring water to new fields. Dig canals, shape the land, and expand what you can grow.
  • Venture Into the Desert – Step beyond the Nile’s reach and cross the sands to discover hidden oases, ruins, and ancient tombs. Carry water and look for shade as you head into the open desert. Search for oases, explore ruins, and descend into tombs to uncover rare materials and lost knowledge buried beneath the sand. What you bring back changes what you can craft and build back along the Nile.
  • Appease the Gods – Make offerings to the gods to earn blessings and unlock new powers. Uncover shrines scattered across the world and bring offerings to earn the gods’ favor. Each god offers their own set of boons. Ptah empowers your tools and craftwork, Hapi speeds up crop growth and makes harvests more bountiful, and Sekhmet grants protection and combat prowess for the desert and its dangers.
  • Discover New Lands – Cross the Nile to reach new regions with brand-new landscapes, mysteries, and treasures. Leave the familiar riverbanks behind and explore new lands where the sands turn white and pyramids rise from the horizon. Restore ancient obelisks to awaken their power, creating new ways to travel between distant regions.

Watch the announcement trailer below. View the first screenshots at the gallery.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/ancient-egypt-set-cozy-open-world-life-simulation-game-fields-of-aaru-announced-for-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Mamon King coming to PS5 on March 20
item.content:encoded:
• PS5

Monster-raising simulation game currently available for Switch and PC.

LiTMUS will release monster-raising simulation game Mamon King for PlayStation 5 on March 20, the company announced. It is available now in regions where it is already March 20.

Mamon King first launched for Switch and PC via Steam on December 11, 2025.

Here is an overview of the game, via PlayStation Store:

About

Players are special beings who can connect with Mamons (monsters) summoned from another world. Raise and train your Mamons with kindness, and sometimes strictness!

After winning countless tournaments, when you defeat the strongest Mamon, you will become the world’s king: the Mamon King!

The monster-raising game set on a ranch has evolved!

Experience a new kind of raising in Mamon King!

Key Features

  • 38 Unique Mamons – Mammon boast distinctive appearances, each excelling in different training areas and possessing varying lifespans. Grasping their traits is your first step to victory.
  • Over 170 Skills – Mamon’s signature skills go beyond mere attack and defense, creating deep strategic depth. Master evolving skills through repeated use to forge even more powerful weapons.
  • Experience the Ultimate Tactical Battle in One-on-One Command Combat – It’s not just about attacking, defending, buffing, or debuffing. Using skills consumes SP, and more powerful skills demand greater SP—or even greater sacrifices. Timing your moves will decide your fate. Experience unparalleled tactical depth in this one-on-one command-based battle system, designed to be accessible yet offering the most intense strategic gameplay.
  • Strengthen Your Mamon Through Training, Tournaments, and Expeditions – Training isn’t the only way to hone your Mamon—it’s also about deciding what to do each week at home. Mamon grows stronger not only through tournaments against rivals but also via Expeditions, where you advance through uncharted lands rumored to hold ancient Mamon in a board game-style format.
  • Your Unique Mamon – A Mamon’s potential blossoms through your skill and luck. Inheritance Summoning passes on the power of your trained Mamon. And with help from a shady scientist or a muscle-bound trainer, your Mamon might gain powers beyond its natural limits?


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/mamon-king-coming-to-ps5-on-march-20
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Basketball Classics now available for PS5, Xbox Series, PS4, Xbox One, and Switch
item.content:encoded:
• PS4
  • Switch
  • Xbox One
  • Xbox Series

Previously released for PC.

8-bit basketball game Basketball Classics is now available for Xbox Series, PlayStation 4, Xbox One, and Switch for $9.99 with a 10 percent-off launch discount, publisher Acclaim and developer Namo Gamo announced.

Basketball Classics first launched in Early Access for PC via Steam on March 24, 2018, followed by a full release on December 18, 2019. The PC version is also now available via Microsoft Store.

“This has been a childhood dream of ours,” said Namo Gamo co-founder David Pilkington in a press release. “Now, almost a decade in the making, Basketball Classics will be available to new users on consoles worldwide. Thanks to Acclaim for making this dream a reality. We can’t wait to follow this up with Super Basketball Classics.”

Here is an overview of the game, via Acclaim:

Five-on-five professional basketball born from the golden era of sports games. This spiritual successor to the 8-bit ballers bridges the gap between retro and today’s simulation games.

  • Side-scrolling, sweaty arcade action.
  • Simplified three-button gameplay.
  • Decades of rosters with over 175 teams.
  • Over 1,000 players with individualized attributes.
  • Seamless play calling.
  • Sensational cut scenes.
  • One-player Story Mode unlocks secret teams.
  • Scream-inducing local multiplayer.
  • Retro vibes galore.
  • No virtual currency. No pay-to-win. No micro transactions. Ever.

Side-scrolling, arcade action is paired with on-the-fly strategy. Three-button gameplay is easy to pick up, but it is the dynamic play-calling that elevates this title beyond just long bombs and acrobatics.

Attribute-rich players make up a vast set of rosters. Dig through decades of teams to find your favorite All-Stars and even unlock the “Legends” in an immersive story mode.

This game is a passion project for us indie developers here at Namo Gamo. We hope you can feel the love put into every pixel, the endless adjustments to stat-driven gameplay, and the genuinely retro sound and music.

Watch a new trailer below.

Consoles Launch Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/basketball-classics-now-available-for-ps5-xbox-series-ps4-xbox-one-and-switch
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Two Point Museum x Angry Birds collaboration update now available
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch 2
  • Xbox Series

New spring seasonal content and quality-of-life updates also available.

Publisher SEGA and developer Two Point Studios have released the version 8.0 update for Two Point Museum, which brings Rovio’s Angry Birds to the museum’s Digiverse, the companies announced. The update is available now for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC, and is coming soon to Switch 2.

Here is an overview of the update, via SEGA:

The Digiverse is an ever-expanding expedition map in Two Point Museum that allows players to explore new Points of Interest, exhibits and decorations from different universes.

The latest rift will ruffle some feathers as Angry Birds slingshots into Two Point County. Players will be able to employ Red and the gang to help excite bird-loving guests and fend off mischievous Bad Piggies from trotting all over the exhibits. The rift will also open up new areas such as Piggy Island, where explorers can discover artefacts from the Angry Birds universe and set up the first ever bird wildlife exhibit. Full details can be found in the blog post on Steam and G2G.

Alongside the Angry Birds Digiverse Rift, curators will be able to give their museums a spring clean with a seasonal update that includes brand new items to freshen up the halls. There will be adventures to embark on with new Points of Interest in the Easter Isles, where players can discover some fluffy critters that have come out of hibernation, helping encourage a new visitor to attend their exhibition—the Egg Hunters.

Available now, the free update will also be jam-packed with new content and quality-of-life changes. Well-seasoned explorers will be able to get new Curator-Class rewards for higher levels of museum management, outdoor items such as Picnic Tables will arrive in time for warmer weather and Briny Bay will now be full of marine life for water exhibits. All of this and more will help managers keep their museum busy and bright for spring.

  • Angry Birds Update
    • Explore Piggy Island, visiting three new Points of Interest: Cobalt Plateaus, Bamboo Forest and Pig City.
    • Dress your guests with Angry Birds merch with new Gift Shop items.
    • Collect nine exhibits, including an interactive display and new roaming Wildlife exhibits.
    • Decorate with new items, wallpaper and flooring to create your very own Angryverse Museum.
  • Spring Seasonal Update
    • Embark to two new Points of Interest that make up the Easter Isles, hidden away in Scorched Earth.
    • Explore three new exhibits, including a cute and fluffy Habitat critter and Botany discoveries.
    • Make your museum blossom with new décor items and customization options for your walls and floor.
    • Lure in customers with new Chocolate Cake Kiosks and baskets of chocolate eggs.

Two Point Museum is available now for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, and PC via Steam.

Watch a new set of trailers below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

Angry Birds Collaboration Trailer

Spring Seasonal Update Trailer

Version 8.0 Update Trailer

Screenshots

  • ❮
  • ❯


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/03/two-point-museum-x-angry-birds-collaboration-update-now-available
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
